vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #670

plan.title: task migrate:vbrief produces non-prettier-clean output, breaks downstream task check

narratives.Description: Running `task migrate:vbrief` emits files that can fail a consumer's `prettier --check`, so a freshly-migrated repo hits a baseline `task check` failure with no guidance on how to fix it. Because migration is a rare one-shot pre-v0.20 legacy path, this story takes the minimum-effort route: emit a post-migration remediation breadcrumb so the surprise failure becomes a known one-command fix, rather than byte-matching prettier in the generator.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
- In the scripts/migrate_vbrief.py module, append a remediation note to the migration completion summary on stdout and to vbrief/migration/LEGACY-REPORT.md that names the generated files and says to run `prettier --write` on them or add them to .prettierignore before `task check`.
- Enumerate the specific generated paths (SPECIFICATION.md, PROJECT.md, ROADMAP.md, vbrief/specification.vbrief.json, vbrief/PROJECT-DEFINITION.vbrief.json, vbrief/migration/LEGACY-REPORT.md) in the note.
- Add a regression test under tests/ that runs the migrator on a fixture and asserts the breadcrumb text appears in the output.
narratives.UserStory: As a consumer migrating a pre-v0.20 project, I want a clear remediation note when migration output is not prettier-clean, so that I can unblock task check with one command instead of debugging a surprise failure.
narratives.Overview:
## Summary

`task migrate:vbrief` emits files that fail `prettier --check`, so any downstream repo that runs the documented `task check` pre-commit gate will be blocked immediately after migration.

## Repro

Discovered while running the v0.20.0 migration on `deftai/bridge` (commit `ffafbee` in that repo's history, run via `task migrate:vbrief`). The migration produced these files in non-prettier-clean form:

- `PROJECT.md`
- `ROADMAP.md`
- `SPECIFICATION.md`
- `vbrief/migration/LEGACY-REPORT.md`
- `vbrief/specification.vbrief.json`

Running `pnpm exec prettier --check` (or `task fmt:check`, which is the documented bridge gate) against any of these flagged them. `prettier --write` then made byte-level changes (whitespace / line-break / JSON-indent normalisations); the diff was purely cosmetic.

## Impact

- Every consumer that adopts the bridge-style `task check` gate (fmt:check + lint + typecheck + test) hits a baseline failure on a fresh post-migration checkout.
- Contributors and agents are tempted to either (a) bypass the gate, or (b) silently re-format the auto-generated files, which then drifts back the next time the migrator runs.
- The bridge scope vBRIEF [`vbrief/proposed/eval-build-fix-migrate-vbrief-prettier-output.vbrief.json`](https://github.com/deftai/bridge/blob/main/vbrief/proposed/eval-build-fix-migrate-vbrief-prettier-output.vbrief.json) tracks the downstream-side problem.

## Workaround already applied in deftai/bridge

PR [deftai/bridge#8](https://github.com/deftai/bridge/pull/8) ran `prettier --write` on the five offending files and excluded `.planning/evaluation/` from prettier so auto-generated metrics snapshots cannot regress the gate. This unblocked `task check` for the bridge repo, but the fix doesn't survive the next run of `task migrate:vbrief` — the next migration would emit the same non-prettier-clean output and re-break the gate.

## Suggested fix (one of)

1. **Generator-side fix (preferred):** make the migration generator emit byte-identical output to what `prettier --write` would produce — match prettier's defaults for Markdown line wrapping and JSON indentation (2 spaces, trailing newline, no BOM).
2. **Post-process step in `task migrate:vbrief`:** after the generator runs, invoke `prettier --write` on the produced artifacts inside the same task so the migration leaves the working tree in a fmt:check-clean state.
3. **Regression gate:** whichever path is taken, add a CI/test step that runs `task migrate:vbrief` against a fixture project and asserts `task fmt:check` exits 0 on the result.

Either (1) or (2) resolves the bug; (3) prevents regression.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Running `task migrate:vbrief` on a fresh checkout produces output that satisfies `prettier --check` with the consumer repo's prettier config.
- [ ] Regression gate added so this stays fixed.

## Source conversation

Discovered during a Warp agent session: <https://app.warp.dev/conversation/44b7d687-f607-4e31-adeb-42aebab82ca4>
narratives.Approach: Scoped down to MINIMUM-EFFORT after 2026-06-16 investigation. Migration is now a rare, one-shot pre-v0.20 legacy path, so byte-matching prettier is over-investment. Empirical finding (running the current migrator on a fixture + isolating bridge PR #8's prettier-write commit ea7b4144): the migrator's own output is already deterministically clean for whitespace/newlines/tabs/CRLF/JSON-indent. The dominant residual delta is JSON single-element-array collapsing (`"dependencies": [\n  "1.1.1"\n]` -> `"dependencies": ["1.1.1"]`) which Python json.dumps(indent=2) always expands and prettier collapses based on printWidth -- consumer-config-dependent and hard to match generator-side. Markdown deltas are trivial spacing (blank line after HTML-comment blocks / before lists; strip trailing blank). DECISION: do NOT fix the formatting. Emit a post-migration remediation breadcrumb so the rare migrating consumer turns a surprise baseline `task check` failure into a known one-command fix. Generator byte-matching (issue option 1) and auto-running prettier (option 2) are OUT OF SCOPE.

narrative.Acceptance: Given a completed migration, when the migrator finishes, then it emits a remediation note on stdout and in vbrief/migration/LEGACY-REPORT.md explaining how to make the output prettier-clean before task check.

Given the remediation note, when a consumer reads it, then it lists the specific generated files (SPECIFICATION.md, PROJECT.md, ROADMAP.md, the two vbrief JSON files, and LEGACY-REPORT.md) to run prettier --write on.
Given the migrator run against a fixture, when the regression test runs, then it asserts the prettier remediation breadcrumb appears in the output and fails if it is removed.

expected_outputs: scripts/migrate_vbrief.py emits a prettier remediation breadcrumb on stdout and in LEGACY-REPORT.md
breadcrumb enumerates the generated file paths
passing regression test asserting the breadcrumb appears
swarm.missing_traces_justification: Bug fix ingested from GitHub issue #670; no spec-section requirement trace applies. Generator byte-matching (option 1) and auto-running prettier (option 2) are intentionally out of scope.

references.title: Issue #670: task migrate:vbrief produces non-prettier-clean output, breaks downstream task check
